phases.goal: Define your travel vision and build the financial runway

steps.title: Define your travel vision precisely
steps.description: Answer these: Where do you want to go first? How long do you want to travel? Are you going nomadic (location-independent income) or planning a fixed trip? What's your monthly budget on the road? Clarity here determines every other decision.
steps.success_metric: Travel vision document written with destination, duration, and budget

steps.title: Calculate your travel fund target
steps.description: Add up: flights, first month's accommodation, travel insurance, visa fees, buffer fund (20% extra). Research your destination's cost of living. Many people over-estimate what travel costs — Southeast Asia or Eastern Europe are often cheaper than home.
steps.success_metric: Specific savings target calculated with itemised breakdown

steps.title: Cut costs to fund your trip
steps.description: Identify your top 3 monthly expenses that can be reduced. Temporarily eliminating subscriptions, eating out less, or downsizing can generate £500–£1,000/month towards your travel fund faster than you think.
steps.success_metric: At least £300/month redirected to travel fund

phases.goal: Sort your practicalities and create income that travels with you

steps.title: Negotiate remote work or build a portable income stream
steps.description: If employed, research whether your role can be done remotely and make the case to your manager. If going freelance, identify one marketable skill (writing, design, code, VA work) and land 2–3 clients before you leave. Don't quit before the income exists.
steps.success_metric: Remote work approved or first freelance client secured

steps.title: Handle the practicalities
steps.description: Get travel insurance that covers health, theft, and cancellation. Notify your bank. Get an international debit card (Wise or Revolut) with no foreign transaction fees. Renew passport if needed. These feel boring but blocking one is trip-ending.

steps.title: Book your first destination
steps.description: Commit by booking. One flight, one week of accommodation. The act of booking transforms travel from dream to plan. Everything else becomes concrete once there's a date.

phases.goal: Execute your first trip and build systems for long-term travel

steps.title: Build your travel workflow
steps.description: Create a packing list that fits in a carry-on. Set up your work environment (VPN, noise-cancelling headphones, portable hotspot). Find one reliable workspace in your destination city. The first trip teaches you what you actually need.
steps.success_metric: Packing list finalised and work setup tested

steps.title: Debrief and plan your next move
steps.description: After your first trip, review: What worked? What didn't? What was the actual cost vs estimate? What would you pack differently? Use this to refine your system for the next destination.
steps.success_metric: Debrief document written and next destination shortlisted
